always: Use the Prisma singleton in lib/db.ts — never instantiate PrismaClient directly in route handlers
Add 'postinstall': 'prisma generate' to package.json — Prisma types must be regenerated after npm install
Run prisma generate after every schema.prisma change before touching TypeScript code
Use prisma.$transaction() for any write that touches more than one table
Use select (not include) on performance-critical queries — only fetch columns you actually use
Add import 'server-only' to lib/db.ts to prevent accidental client-side Prisma imports
Use findUniqueOrThrow / findFirstOrThrow instead of findUnique when the record must exist
Add DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL to .env — DIRECT_URL bypasses PgBouncer for migrations
never: Never use prisma db push in production — it has no migration history and can cause drift
Never instantiate new PrismaClient() outside of lib/db.ts — each instance opens a new connection pool
Never use both select and include on the same query level — Prisma rejects it
Never edit prisma/migrations/ files manually — regenerate with prisma migrate dev instead
Never run prisma migrate dev in production — use prisma migrate deploy
Never soft-delete without filtering deletedAt: null everywhere — it is trivially easy to forget
Never change a Prisma enum by removing a value without a migration — it is a breaking schema change
gotchas: Hot reload in Next.js dev creates multiple PrismaClient instances and exhausts the connection pool — use the singleton pattern: const prisma = global.prisma ?? new PrismaClient(); if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') (global as any).prisma = prisma
Serverless / edge functions open a new connection per invocation — use Prisma Accelerate or PgBouncer (set connection_limit=1 in DATABASE_URL) or you will hit max_connections quickly
prisma migrate dev requires a shadow database — hosted Postgres (Supabase, Neon) may need shadowDatabaseUrl set in schema.prisma, or use --skip-seed flag
@updatedAt auto-updates on update() but NOT on updateMany() or raw queries — bulk updates silently skip the timestamp
N+1 queries are invisible by default — enable query logging in dev: new PrismaClient({ log: ['query'] }) and check for repeated queries in loops
prisma generate output goes to node_modules — it must be re-run after npm ci in CI/CD or types will be stale
Prisma enums in PostgreSQL are native DB enums — adding a value requires a migration, removing one is destructive; prefer String + a Zod enum for values that change often
Relations in Prisma are not fetched by default — a User query never includes Posts unless you add include: { posts: true } or select them explicitly
prisma.$transaction() with an array runs in parallel inside one transaction; the callback form (async tx => {}) runs sequentially — use the callback form for dependent writes
DIRECT_URL is required when using a connection pooler (PgBouncer, Supabase pooler) — DATABASE_URL points to pooler, DIRECT_URL points to direct DB; migrate commands need DIRECT_URL

folders.lib/db.ts: Prisma singleton — the only place PrismaClient is instantiated
folders.prisma/schema.prisma: single source of truth for schema — never edit migrations directly
folders.prisma/migrations/: auto-generated migration history — commit all files, never edit
folders.prisma/seed.ts: database seed script — run with: npx prisma db seed
folders.app/api/: Route Handlers — import db from lib/db, never import Prisma client directly
